div.p: 1. Recursive Invocation Model Command substitution is implemented as a recursive shell evaluation. The subshell (Child) performs a full lex-parse-eval loop. This supports:

ul.li: Arbitrary Pipelines: \$(cat file | grep pattern | awk '{print \$1}')

Parent Responsibility: The parent process must close(fd[1]) immediately after fork(). If the parent keeps the write end open, a read(fd[0]) will block indefinitely even after the child exits, as the pipe never receives an End-of-File (EOF).
Child Responsibility: The child must close(fd[0]) to prevent descriptor leakage and ensure resource cleanup.

div.p: 3. Output Normalization As per POSIX.1-2017, the shell captures standard output.

ul.li: Captured string undergoes trailing newline removal.
Internal newlines are preserved within the expanded word.
The resulting string is subject to field splitting if not double-quoted.

g.text: Note: Map pipe write-end to STDOUT
